methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Christian Rood and Gabriel de Scheemaker, having raised €23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-sector, same-stage companies, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA) and eight additional private peers in the Climate & Energy space.

The peer-median method, the primary applicable triangulation approach, yields a valuation of $355M based on the median of six direct comparables. The peer universe ranges from $120M (floor) to $5.0B (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, an outlier), with the five-company sample including Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M). This median reflects typical early-growth venture funding valuations in the energy transition sector at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on $355M. This relatively tight 35% band reflects consistency within the peer cohort once outliers are factored into the low and high bounds. Key uncertainties include LeydenJar's specific technology differentiation, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the timing and economics of commercialization—factors that could materially shift valuation within or beyond the stated range. The single-method approach also limits triangulation robustness; revenue multiples or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen conviction if applicable data become available.
